summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic `Highlight` utility component (task #204) for ars-ui, covering text-to-chunks splitting with Contains/StartsWith/Fuzzy strategies and Unicode-aware case folding, then harden it through spec-drift audits, test coverage analysis, a reusable post-implementation-audit skill, and PR review resolution.
summary.outcome: PR #647 opened with `Closes #204`, including the full `highlight.rs` module, TR21 case folding via a new `ars_i18n::case_fold` helper, 44 unit tests, 9 insta snapshots, proptest invariants, spec-conformance test, 0 missed mutations, and a new mandatory `post-implementation-audit` skill committed alongside the implementation.

repo: `ars-components` declares `ars-i18n = { default-features = false }`, so any `ars_i18n` feature-gated function (e.g., `to_lowercase`, `case_fold`) requires an explicit forwarding feature in `ars-components/Cargo.toml` — the plan's 'no Cargo.toml changes' assumption was wrong

`ars_i18n::to_lowercase` wraps `CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string` and preserves `ß` — it does NOT expand it to `ss`. TR21 case folding (`fold_string`) is required for genuine `ß ↔ ss ↔ SS` equivalence; using `to_lowercase` silently violates the German eszett contract

`cargo llvm-cov` reports `0.00%` for wasm-gated code paths — not uncovered but unmeasured. Adapter crates and wasm-only paths require `cargo xtask coverage wasm --package <crate> --feature <f> --file out.lcov.info`; never use bare llvm-cov for those crates

Rust `str::match_indices` does NOT find overlapping matches — e.g., query `'aa'` in `'aaa'` yields one match, not two. Contains strategy requires a manual loop advancing by one byte after each hit to find all overlapping occurrences
Fuzzy matching with multi-char fold expansions (e.g., `İ → i + combining dot`) must match per original source character, not per folded byte, to avoid partial-char boundary violations
`.claude/skills/` is a symlink to `.agents/skills/` — the canonical path for skills and the correct path to reference in AGENTS.md is `.agents/skills/`
CLAUDE.md is itself a symlink to AGENTS.md — edits must target `AGENTS.md` directly

`cargo mutants` timeout mutations (infinite loops from `+` → no-advance mutations) count as caught, not missed; they should be added to `mutants.toml` skip entries only when they are true equivalents, not timeouts

The spec template (`foundation/10` §4.1.2) requires `Part` + `Api` + `ConnectApi` for all stateless components even when a spec explicitly says 'function-based API' — resolve by implementing both the standalone function and the thin attribute-mapper `Api`

Anatomy slots whose attributes depend on runtime data (parametric slots like `Chunk`) cannot be `Part` enum variants when `ConnectApi::part_attrs` takes `Part` by value with no payload — expose via a parameterised `Api::method(bool)` instead
Adapter feature wiring: `ars-leptos/icu4x` and `ars-dioxus/icu4x` features must explicitly forward `ars-components/i18n` or the Highlight module is invisible to adapter builds even when icu4x is on

code.finding: New file: Props (with builder), MatchStrategy (Contains/StartsWith/Fuzzy), HighlightChunk, Part, Api, ConnectApi, highlight_chunks, full test suite. Gated on `#[cfg(feature = "i18n")]`.
`pub mod highlight;` registration gated on `#[cfg(feature = "i18n")]` — module is absent from non-i18n builds rather than providing a fallback

Added `case_fold(text, locale)` wrapping `CaseMapper::fold_turkic_string` for Turkic locales and `CaseMapper::fold_string` otherwise — this is TR21, not `lowercase_to_string`, and is the correct function for case-insensitive matching (expands ß→ss, collapses Greek final sigma)
Proptest block added: `proptest_highlight_chunks_invariants` fuzz-tests (props × locale) across 8 locales including `az`, `lt`, `hy` for broader Unicode coverage; 5 invariants checked
New spec-conformance test: asserts `Part::scope() == "highlight"` and anatomy table matches `Part::all()` — build-time anatomy-drift detection per workspace convention

Skip entries for equivalent mutations (defensive zero-length guards, `orig_start < orig_end` and `orig_end > 0` filters) and for timeout-producing `+` mutations in `push_contains_matches` advance loop

New §4.2 'Static vs parametric anatomy slots' section documenting the convention that anatomy rows whose attributes depend on runtime data use a parameterised `Api` method instead of a `Part` enum variant

workflow: The plan's case-folding decision ('use `to_lowercase`, identical to fold on named tests') was incorrect — the eszett test in the plan only covered uppercase-to-lowercase direction and silently missed the `ß ↔ ss` contract. Always verify Unicode folding claims with a live probe, not just reasoning about named test cases

Three-phase post-implementation audit (spec drift → iterative surface scan → test coverage) reliably surfaces issues that TDD + acceptance criteria alone miss; codified as the mandatory `post-implementation-audit` skill in `.agents/skills/`

`cargo mutants` should be re-run after any algorithmic change to the module — the new `Normalised::map_range` design eliminated two previously-documented equivalent mutations, and the Contains advance loop introduced three new timeout mutations
Running `cargo xci` in the background and working on other tasks (spec edits, mutants run) while waiting prevents idle time in the pre-PR gate

Snapshot tests must be regenerated with `cargo insta accept` whenever the output changes (e.g., adding `dir="auto"` to `root_attrs`) — always re-run with `--unreferenced=reject` before committing to catch orphan snapshots

When both spec and implementation are wrong (e.g., the eszett case), escalate to the user via `AskUserQuestion` before landing — the fix touches all three (spec + impl + tests) and needs design confirmation

External skill analyzers (ambiguity-detection, cognitive-load) catch real issues in workflow skills but apply generic templates — evaluate each finding on its specific merit before applying the suggested fix mechanically

friction: The approved plan stated 'No Cargo.toml changes' but enabling `ars_i18n::case_fold` required adding an `i18n = ["ars-i18n/icu4x"]` feature — an assumption should have been verified against the dependency graph before writing the plan

The case-folding decision made during planning (use `to_lowercase` over `fold_string`) was wrong for German eszett; the error was only discovered during the third audit round, requiring a retroactive switch to `case_fold` and new helper in `ars-i18n`
Background task notifications were unreliable — `cargo xci` completed without a notification arriving, requiring the user to ask 'Did xci finish?' to unblock the session
The skill file was initially written to `.claude/skills/` (a symlink) instead of the canonical `.agents/skills/` path, requiring a corrective AGENTS.md edit
Rust `match_indices` not finding overlapping matches was a hidden semantic gap in the Contains strategy that only surfaced as a PR review comment, not during initial implementation or testing
open_items: Issue #204 remains 'In Progress' on the project board — must move to 'Done' only after PR #647 merges and CI passes
Leptos and Dioxus adapter wrapper implementations for Highlight (the `<Highlight>` components that consume `highlight_chunks`) are out of scope for this task and will land in future adapter tasks
Prelude re-exports for Highlight in `ars-leptos` and `ars-dioxus` land with the adapter implementation tasks

Performance optimization for per-char `case_fold` allocation in `build_normalised` is deferred — no streaming `CaseMapper` API exists in ICU4X 2.x, so the per-char allocation is the only correct approach until upstream changes

The 'parametric anatomy slot' pattern (documented inline in Highlight spec and `foundation/10`) should be illustrated with concrete examples from table rows, tags-input chips, and toaster toasts when those components are implemented — a broader cross-cutting foundation update deferred

The one remaining uncovered region (defensive `else` of `if end > start` zero-length-highlight guard in `build_chunks`) is documented as an equivalent mutation in `mutants.toml` — intentionally left at 99.91% rather than adding a misleading test to fake the unreachable branch
